234.reason: [After detecting a single '&' or '|', the function returns early without consuming the character properly in all cases, and more critically, it does not check bounds before accessing curString.charAt(pos+1), which can cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' is the last character in the line. Additionally, even if an error is detected, the code still attempts to add a token after this block, leading to a NullPointerException when type is null.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [After detecting a single '&' or '|', the function returns early without consuming the character properly in all cases, and more critically, it does not check bounds before accessing curString.charAt(pos+1), which can cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' is the last character in the line. Additionally, even if an error is detected, the code still attempts to add a token after this block, leading to a NullPointerException when type is null.]

248.reason: [If a single '&' or '|' triggers an error and returns early, this line is skipped. However, if the error flag was already set previously, this block may still try to process an invalid symbol (like "&") that is not in keyToToken, causing type to be null, resulting in a NullPointerException when calling type.toString().]
